what_it_does: This file is a channel-memory attachment metadata record for the design-source channel; it declares an images attachment with a unique id, a target image URI, and the timestamp it was added.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
standouts: The target URI points to a pitch-deck image (p-16) in the design-source namespace.
The attachment id uses the 'att-' prefix pattern.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
Channels are named with a 'channel:' field.
Media targets use a custom 'image://' URI scheme.
Timestamps are ISO 8601 with timezone offset.
concerns: The attachment record lacks a caption, title, or description of the image content.
No media size, dimensions, mime type, or checksum are recorded.
It is not clear from the file alone which message or entry references this attachment.
notes: A bare attachment provenance record that links an 'att-' id to a pitch-deck image URI in the design-source channel.
questions: What visual content does image://design-source/pitch-deck/p-16 contain?
Which channel message or entry is this attachment associated with?
Who added the attachment and why?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file's metadata fields describe an image attachment record, and the content does exactly that.
intention_signals: To create a durable, referenceable record that an image attachment was added to the design-source channel and to bind it to a stable image URI for retrieval.
novelty: The use of a custom image:// URI scheme namespaced by channel and collection (pitch-deck/p-16) to address media assets.
contribution: It contributes a lightweight, timestamped attachment-to-URI mapping that lets a fused system locate and audit a design-source channel image without storing the binary asset inline.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML attachment metadata record with id att-8bb06a7b in the design-source channel, declaring an images attachment that targets image://design-source/pitch-deck/p-16 and recording its addition timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog an image attachment in the design-source channel so it can be referenced by a stable URI and its addition time.
